expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid banding scenarios distinguish styling variants, interactions, and backward-compatible rendering outcomes
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the output explicitly states the benchmark focus and then enumerates coverage sections for all three areas: “explicitly covers modern grid banding scenarios—distinguishing styling variants, interactions, and backward-compatible rendering outcomes” and provides dedicated subsections “1) Styling variants”, “2) Interactions”, and “3) Backward-compatible rendering outcomes”.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The output repeatedly anchors to Phase 4a and its constraints: result.md says “Primary phase/checkpoint under test: Phase 4a (subcategory-only draft writer)” and lists Phase 4a rules (subcategory-first, scenario→atomic chain→observable leaves, avoid Phase 4b canonical groupings). execution_notes.md also states “Output kept explicitly aligned to Phase 4a ... avoided Phase 4b canonical top-layer categories.”
